commit.message:
fix: sesion definitiva — logout nuclear + SessionProvider sin session prop

Dos causas raíz identificadas:

1. SessionProvider recibía session={session} del servidor. Si esa sesión
   quedaba "pegada" en el estado del cliente, cambios de cuenta mostraban
   datos del usuario anterior. Solución: SessionProvider sin prop session;
   la sesión real se lee siempre en el servidor via auth().

2. El logout usaba NextResponse.redirect (307) con Set-Cookie. Algunos
   proxies/CDN ignoran los Set-Cookie en respuestas de redirect.
   Solución: response 200 OK con <script>window.location.replace("/login")
   que navega DESPUÉS de que el browser procesa todos los Set-Cookie.
   Además se borran TODOS los cookies del request (no solo los nombrados),
   garantizando limpieza completa sin importar variaciones de nombre.

commit.message:
fix(evidence): add SQL query files for DuckDB source table discovery

Evidence DuckDB plugin does not auto-discover tables without .sql files
in the source directory. Without them, npm run sources connects to the
DuckDB file but extracts zero tables — causing "Table traces does not
exist" during build.

Add explicit SQL files for each table materialized by materialize.py:
traces, spans, guardrail_events, scores, mart_eval_drift, budget_windows.

Add project README and test notes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Phase 1 - Project Foundation & User System (reviewed & approved)

Implements the complete Phase 1 foundation including all code review remediations:

Core features:
- Next.js 16 App Router with TypeScript strict mode, TailwindCSS v4, Shadcn UI
- PostgreSQL via Supabase with Prisma 7 ORM and adapter-pg driver
- Auth.js v5 credentials provider with JWT sessions and RBAC (USER/ADMIN)
- User registration, login with rate limiting, profile management
- Student ID upload to private Supabase Storage bucket (signed URLs)
- Admin panel: grade options CRUD, reorder, system settings toggle
- Responsive layout with Header, Sidebar navigation

Security & architecture remediations:
- Admin seed password from ADMIN_SEED_PASSWORD env var (no hardcoded credentials)
- Business logic extracted into src/server/services/ (layered architecture)
- Domain error hierarchy: AppError base + 6 typed subclasses + mapErrorToResponse()
- Atomic student ID upload: upload-new → update-DB (rollback) → delete-old
- Prisma P2002 handling for username and grade label unique constraints
- Server-side grade validation against active GradeOption records
- TypeScript strict mode passing with zero tsc --noEmit errors
- Postgres-backed rate limiter via LoginAttempt model (replaces in-memory Map)
- Middleware migrated to Next.js 16 proxy convention (src/proxy.ts)

Infrastructure:
- LoginAttempt migration (prisma/migrations/20260311015109_add_login_attempts)
- /api/health endpoint with DB + rate-limiter table existence checks
- Integration test suite for rate limiter against real DB (5 tests)
- 49 unit tests, 5 integration tests, all passing

commit.message:
Phase 3: ScenarioRunner, MockReceiver, and CLI

Wire all Phase 2 components together into a full test orchestration pipeline:

- core/scenario_runner.py: ScenarioRunner orchestrates the complete test flow
  (pre-generate audio, trigger call, wait for connection, run steps with
  turn detection, hang up, fetch post-call data). DI pattern for all deps.
  Error handling for call timeout, step timeout, and mid-call disconnect
  with partial result capture.

- receivers/mock_receiver.py: MockReceiver implements CallReceiver for testing
  without Twilio. Feeds synthetic agent audio (speech + silence) and captures
  sent audio for verification.

- cli.py: Minimal argparse CLI supporting:
  python cli.py run --scenario <path> --mock
  python cli.py run --scenario-dir <dir> --mock

- tests/test_scenario_runner.py: 18 tests covering MockReceiver, full mock
  e2e scenario, call timeout, step timeout, partial results on disconnect,
  StepResult field verification, CLI argument parsing, scenario loading,
  and CLI integration e2e.

All 129 tests pass (111 existing + 18 new, zero regressions).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add import_recipe tool with AppleScript UI automation

Automates Paprika's built-in browser to scrape and save recipes from
URLs. Uses AppleScript to drive the UI flow: navigate to Browser,
set URL, click Download to scrape, then Save to persist. Detects
newly imported recipes via DB polling and optionally tags them with
categories using the existing kill/write/restart pattern.

feat: add balanced sampling and raw labels to BasinLabeledDataset

- Add balanced sampling strategy with per-basin quotas and max attempts

  distribution counts for imbalanced systems like Kuramoto

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: increase competitive_lv INTERACTION_SCALE to 0.70 for 4 basins

The old default (0.35) produced only 1 observed basin, making all
competitive_lv benchmark results trivial. Scale 0.70 yields 4 major
basins with meaningful species-extinction diversity.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: update project instructions for paper wrap-up phase

Add PAPER_TRACK_STATUS.md to the documentation stack, clarify the
three-doc workflow (paper track → experiment log → manuscript), and
add wrap-up phase directives for both CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ci): isolate DuckDB and Databricks source runs to prevent cache wipe

The second npm run sources was re-processing agentic_commerce (DuckDB),
and when Databricks failed, it wiped the entire parquet cache — including
the DuckDB data cached in the first run.

Fix: hide each source during the other's run so they can't interfere.
DuckDB runs alone first, Databricks runs alone second.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Initial commit - Native Nomads V2 Wix project

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: record Kuramoto mode-support audit results (strong negative)

The Kuramoto unique mode-support audit (30/30 array tasks, collector
complete) confirms that winding-number basins do not have meaningful
basin-specific support patterns. Uniqueness is trivially degenerate:
every trajectory has its own singleton support, Hamming geometry is
flat, and the result is identical across all 3 model families, 5 seeds,
both sampling protocols, all support definitions, and all thresholds.

Updates EXPERIMENTS.md, PAPER_TRACK_STATUS.md, and notes.tex to reflect
the completed audit and close the previously open stronger claim.
